UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores information about a player's profile, including progression, social stats, and personalization settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

displayName.description: The public display name of the player.

email.description: The player's email address, if provided through authentication.

photoUrl.description: URL to the player's profile picture.

winStreak.description: The current consecutive number of games won by the player.

xp.description: Experience points earned by the player, contributing to their level progression.

badgeIds.description: An array of unique identifiers for badges earned by the player.

totalGamesPlayed.description: The total number of games the player has participated in.

totalWins.description: The total number of games won by the player.

totalLosses.description: The total number of games lost by the player.

averageGuessCount.description: The average number of guesses it takes the player to win a game.

lastOnlineAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user was online.

preferredTheme.description: The aesthetic theme chosen by the player (e.g., Neon, Cyberpunk).

sfxEnabled.description: Indicates if sound effects are enabled for the player.

musicEnabled.description: Indicates if background music is enabled for the player.

GameRoom.title: GameRoom
GameRoom.description: Manages the configuration and state of a multiplayer game room where players can join and play.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameRoom entity.

hostId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the room's host. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameRoom)

playerIds.description: References to the UserProfile entities participating in the game room. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N GameRoom)

status.description: The current status of the game room (e.g., waiting for players, in-game, finished, expired, aborted).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the room was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the room's state.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the room is scheduled to expire if inactive.

minNumber.description: The minimum possible number for the guessing range, set by the host.

maxNumber.description: The maximum possible number for the guessing range, set by the host.

turnTimerSeconds.description: The duration in seconds allocated for each player's turn.

maxAttemptsPerPlayer.description: The maximum number of guesses each player is allowed in the game.

suddenDeathMode.description: Indicates if 'Sudden Death' mode is active, where players have fewer attempts or a shorter timer.

currentGameMatchId.description: Reference to the currently active GameMatch entity within this room. (Relationship: GameMatch 1:N GameRoom)

GameMatch.title: GameMatch
GameMatch.description: Represents a single instance of a 'Guess The Number' duel game, tracking its state, player turns, and guesses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameMatch entity.

roomId.description: Reference to the GameRoom in which this match is being played. (Relationship: GameRoom 1:N GameMatch)

player1Id.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the first player in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameMatch)

player2Id.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the second player in the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameMatch)

targetNumber.description: The secret number that players must guess.

status.description: The current status of the game match (e.g., pending, in-progress, finished, aborted).

currentTurnPlayerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile whose turn it currently is. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameMatch)

turnStartTime.description: Server timestamp indicating when the current player's turn began.

guesses.description: A chronological list of all guesses made in the match.

player1AttemptsLeft.description: The number of attempts remaining for Player 1.

player2AttemptsLeft.description: The number of attempts remaining for Player 2.

winnerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the player who won the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameMatch)

loserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the player who lost the match. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N GameMatch)

startTime.description: Timestamp when the match officially started.

lastGuessRangeMin.description: The minimum value of the dynamically narrowed guessing range after the last guess feedback.

lastGuessRangeMax.description: The maximum value of the dynamically narrowed guessing range after the last guess feedback.

Guess.title: Guess
Guess.description: Represents a single guess made by a player in a GameMatch.

playerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made this guess. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Guess)

guess.description: The number guessed by the player.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the guess was submitted.

turnNumber.description: The sequential turn number in the match when this guess was made.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Stores individual chat messages exchanged between players within a game room.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

roomId.description: Reference to the GameRoom where this message was sent. (Relationship: GameRoom 1:N ChatMessage)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

message.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

emojiReaction.description: An optional emoji reaction associated with the message.

LeaderboardEntry.title: LeaderboardEntry
LeaderboardEntry.description: Represents a player's entry on the global leaderboard, summarizing their key performance indicators. This entity is designed for optimized leaderboard display.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeaderboardEntry entity, typically matching the userId.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this leaderboard entry belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 LeaderboardEntry)

displayName.description: The player's display name, denormalized for quick leaderboard rendering.

totalWins.description: The total number of games won by the player, used for ranking.

winStreak.description: The player's current consecutive win streak, used for ranking.

xp.description: The player's experience points, used for ranking.

level.description: The player's current level, used for ranking.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last update to this leaderboard entry.

definition.description: Stores individual player profiles. Each document is owned by a specific user identified by `userId`. Read access is public for general information (e.g., for opponent display), but write access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Manages multiplayer game room configurations and state. `hostId` and `playerIds` (a membership map) fields are used for authorization. Listing is allowed for rooms with `status == 'waitingForPlayers'` (QAP compliant). Includes `hostId` and `playerIds` for authorization.

params.name: gameRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier for a game room.

definition.description: Represents a single instance of a 'Guess The Number' duel game within a room. Includes denormalized `player1Id` and `player2Id` from the parent `GameRoom` to ensure authorization independence without `get()` calls. Only these players can read/update the match state.

params.name: gameRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent game room.

params.name: gameMatchId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific game match within the room.

definition.description: Stores individual guesses made by players in a GameMatch. Includes denormalized `player1Id` and `player2Id` from the parent `GameMatch` for authorization independence. Players can only create their own guesses (`playerId`), and all participating players can read all guesses.

params.name: gameRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent game room.

params.name: gameMatchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent game match.

params.name: guessId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific guess.

definition.description: Stores chat messages exchanged within a game room. Includes denormalized `playerIds` and `hostId` from the parent `GameRoom` for authorization independence. Only players participating in the room can send or read messages.

params.name: gameRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent game room.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific chat message.

definition.description: A public collection for displaying global player rankings. Optimized for listing. Anyone can read, but only trusted backend services can write or update entries, ensuring data integrity. `id` matches `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID, for whom this leaderboard entry is.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust security rules and simplified debugging, adhering to all core design principles and strategy mandates. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
    *   For `GameMatch` documents located at `/gameRooms/{gameRoomId}/gameMatches/{gameMatchId}`, the critical authorization fields `player1Id` and `player2Id` (identifying the active players) are denormalized directly from the conceptual room participants. This ensures that security rules for `GameMatch` can validate user access (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.player1Id || request.auth.uid == resource.data.player2Id`) without performing a `get()` operation on the parent `GameRoom` document. This allows for atomic operations (like creating a room and its first match simultaneously) and prevents rule complexity.
    *   Similarly, for `Guess` documents at `/gameRooms/{gameRoomId}/gameMatches/{gameMatchId}/guesses/{guessId}`, the `player1Id` and `player2Id` from the parent `GameMatch` are denormalized into each `Guess` document. This allows rules to verify that only a participating player can make a guess, and all participating players can read all guesses, again without relying on a `get()` call to the `GameMatch` or `GameRoom`.
    *   For `ChatMessage` documents at `/gameRooms/{gameRoomId}/chatMessages/{chatMessageId}`, the `playerIds` array and `hostId` from the parent `GameRoom` are denormalized into each `ChatMessage`. This enables rules to efficiently check if `request.auth.uid` is part of `resource.data.playerIds` (or `resource.data.hostId`) for both sending and reading messages, maintaining authorization independence.

2.  **QAPs (Secure List Operations):**
    *   **`/userProfiles/{userId}`:** This collection allows authenticated users to read any profile for display purposes (e.g., opponent info, leaderboard). Write access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`). Listing is generally not done on this collection directly but by explicit `userId` or via `leaderboard/{userId}`.
    *   **`/gameRooms/{gameRoomId}`:** This is a crucial collection for discovering active games. By segregating rooms into a top-level collection, we can implement QAP-compliant rules. Rules will explicitly allow `list` operations for documents where `resource.data.status == 'waitingForPlayers'`. This enables players to browse available rooms without exposing private room details or requiring client-side filtering of unauthorized rooms. Once a player is part of a room (i.e., `request.auth.uid in resource.data.playerIds`), they can read and potentially update (if host) the specific room document. The `playerIds` field also acts as a membership map.
    *   **`/leaderboard/{userId}`:** This collection is explicitly designed for public listing (`list` queries) to display global rankings. Security rules will allow anyone to read, but only backend processes (e.g., Cloud Functions) to write/update, ensuring data integrity and allowing QAP-compliant public leaderboard display.
    *   Subcollections like `gameMatches`, `guesses`, and `chatMessages` are not typically 'listed' broadly. Instead, they are accessed within the context of a specific `gameRoomId` and `gameMatchId` by authorized players (whose authorization context is denormalized within the documents themselves).

3.  **Structural Segregation:** Data with differing security postures is separated. For example, `UserProfile` (private owner, public viewable fields) and `LeaderboardEntry` (fully public, backend-managed) are distinct collections, simplifying their respective security rules. Game-related data is nested within `gameRooms`, ensuring a clear hierarchy and scope for access.

4.  **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership (`/userProfiles/{userId}`) is used for private user data. Hierarchical paths (`/gameRooms/{gameRoomId}/gameMatches/{gameMatchId}`) are used for 1:N relationships, with denormalization handling the authorization independence. Collaborative data in `GameRoom` and `ChatMessage` uses membership maps (`playerIds`).

5.  **Data Clarity:** Explicit `status` fields are used in `GameRoom` and `GameMatch` for clear state management. Descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{gameRoomId}`, etc.) are used for path parameters.
